verses.text: The LORD called unto Moses and spake from the tabernacle of the congregation: speak to Israel — when any man brings an offering, bring it of the cattle, of the herd or of the flock. Guidance: Leviticus begins with God calling. The book is not first about rules but about a voice — God speaks from inside the new tent. Israel does not have to climb Sinai; the call comes from the camp.

The LORD called unto Moses and spake from the tabernacle of the congregation: speak to Israel — when any man brings an offering, bring it of the cattle, of the herd or of the flock. Guidance: Leviticus begins with God calling. The book is not first about rules but about a voice — God speaks from inside the new tent. Israel does not have to climb Sinai; the call comes from the camp.

If his offering be a burnt sacrifice of the herd — a male without blemish, at the tabernacle door, voluntarily. Lay his hand on its head — accepted to atone. Kill before the LORD; priests sprinkle blood round the altar. Flay, cut in pieces; head and fat on the wood. Priest burns all — sweet savor. Guidance: Hand on head transfers identity; the bullock dies in his place. The whole animal means whole surrender. Hebrews 10 reads every burnt offering as a small word for what Christ would say fully.

Guidance: Hand on head transfers identity; the bullock dies in his place. The whole animal means whole surrender. Hebrews 10 reads every burnt offering as a small word for what Christ would say fully.

Guidance: Hand on head transfers identity; the bullock dies in his place. The whole animal means whole surrender. If his offering be of the flocks — sheep or goats — a male without blemish. Kill on the north side; priests sprinkle blood round the altar. Cut in pieces with head and fat on the wood. Wash inwards and legs; priest burns all — sweet savor unto the LORD. Guidance: A sheep instead of a bullock, but the same shape: blameless, hand transferred, blood at the altar, smoke rising. Different price, same path. God meets the herdsman and the shepherd alike.

If his offering be of the flocks — sheep or goats — a male without blemish. Kill on the north side; priests sprinkle blood round the altar. Cut in pieces with head and fat on the wood. Wash inwards and legs; priest burns all — sweet savor unto the LORD. Guidance: A sheep instead of a bullock, but the same shape: blameless, hand transferred, blood at the altar, smoke rising. Different price, same path. God meets the herdsman and the shepherd alike.

Wash inwards and legs; priest burns all — sweet savor unto the LORD. Guidance: A sheep instead of a bullock, but the same shape: blameless, hand transferred, blood at the altar, smoke rising. Different price, same path. God meets the herdsman and the shepherd alike.

If his offering be of the flocks — sheep or goats — a male without blemish. Kill on the north side; priests sprinkle blood round the altar. Cut in pieces with head and fat on the wood. Wash inwards and legs; priest burns all — sweet savor unto the LORD. Guidance: A sheep instead of a bullock, but the same shape: blameless, hand transferred, blood at the altar, smoke rising. Different price, same path. God meets the herdsman and the shepherd alike.

If his burnt sacrifice be of fowls — turtledoves or young pigeons. The priest wrings off the head, burns it; blood beside. Plucks crop with feathers, casts east. Cleaves with the wings, not dividing. Burns on the wood — sweet savor unto the LORD. Guidance: A turtledove for those who cannot afford a lamb. God's altar is not means-tested; the poor man's pigeon gives the same sweet savor as the rich man's bullock. Mary and Joseph brought this pair after Jesus was born.
